seoTitle: How to Remove a User in Jira
metaDescription: Learn how to remove a user's access in Jira, whether from a specific project or the entire site, and what happens to their assigned work.
intro: When someone leaves a team or project, cleaning up their access in Jira keeps your user list and permissions accurate. This guide covers removing a user, along with what to consider for issues that were assigned to them.

steps.heading: Decide the scope of removal
steps.body: Determine whether the person needs to be removed from a single project or from the entire Jira site, since the steps differ.

steps.heading: Reassign their open issues
steps.body: Before removing access, reassign any issues currently assigned to that user so work doesn't get orphaned.

steps.heading: Remove them from the project
steps.body: If it's project-level, remove the user from the relevant project role or permission group.

steps.heading: Deactivate or remove the site account
steps.body: For a full site removal, go to user management and deactivate or delete the account, depending on your organization's policy.

steps.heading: Confirm the change
steps.body: Check that the user no longer appears in assignee dropdowns or has access to the relevant projects.

faq.question: What happens to issues assigned to a removed user?
faq.answer: They typically stay assigned to that user unless you manually reassign them first, so it's best to handle reassignment before removing access.

faq.question: Should I deactivate or fully delete a user account?
faq.answer: Deactivating preserves historical data like past comments and assignments, while deleting can remove that association, so deactivating is often the safer choice.

faq.question: Do I need admin permissions to remove a user?
faq.answer: Yes, removing or deactivating users generally requires Jira or organization administrator access.

faq.question: Can a removed user be added back later?
faq.answer: Yes, in most cases a deactivated or removed user can be reinstated or re-invited if they rejoin the team.
